title: Besan Chilla
description: Savory chickpea-flour pancakes loaded with vegetables. A high-protein Indian breakfast staple.
explanation: Besan chilla is one of the most reliable plant protein breakfasts in an Indian kitchen. The batter is just chickpea flour, water, spices, and finely chopped vegetables.
Cook it thin like a crepe for a lighter plate, or slightly thicker for a sturdier wrap. Pair with chutney, soy curd, or a fruit side.
instructions: Whisk besan with water until smooth and pourable, with no lumps.
Stir in turmeric, cumin powder, salt, chilli, tomato, onion (if using), and coriander.
Heat a non-stick or well-seasoned tawa. Lightly oil it.
Pour a ladle of batter and spread into a thin circle.
Cook 2–3 minutes until the underside is golden, flip, and cook the other side.
Serve hot with mint chutney, ketchup, or plant-based curd.
stepNotes: A slightly thinner batter spreads more easily; add water a spoon at a time.
Skip onion for Jain or no-onion versions; add grated carrot, cabbage, or zucchini instead.
Medium heat prevents raw centers and burnt edges.
Do not spread too thick or the middle stays pasty.
Make a stack and reheat lightly if cooking for more people.
Chutney, pickle, or a glass of soy milk turns this into a complete breakfast.

variationSections.title: Protein and calorie boosts
items: Add a spoon of flax or hemp seeds to the batter.
Serve with peanut chutney or soy curd for more protein.
Stuff with tofu scramble or leftover sabzi for a heavier meal.

variationSections.title: Vegetable mix-ins
items: Grated carrot, capsicum, spinach, methi, or cabbage all work well.
Kasuri methi gives a restaurant-style aroma.
Ajwain (carom seeds) aids digestion and adds a classic chilla flavor.
